rationale: Injection hole on this bead's core surface. slug and spec are interpolated RAW into the shell command string at ms-panel.js:78 (`${verb} ${slug} --spec ${specId} --target ${target}`), but validateArgs (204-205) guards them with validatePathElement ONLY — never validateShellSafe. validatePathElement rejects empty/./../ /\/control bytes; it does NOT reject `;` `|` `&` backtick `$(` space. Verified empirically: slug=`x$(rm -rf ~)`, `x;id`, `x|sh`, `x\`id\`` all PASS and produce `mindspec panel create x$(id) --spec ...`. The workflow hands that string to an agent ("Run exactly this command") → Bash → shell substitution/chaining executes. The dash guard does not catch it (payload doesn't start with `-`). This is asymmetric: target (172) AND bead_id (143) get validateShellSafe precisely "since they flow into a built command line" (plan L410) — slug/spec flow into the SAME line 78 and were omitted. buildCommand round-3 reconciliation (fixed template flags, guard on values only), ALLOWED_CLI exact-4/no-`mindspec complete`, and the anti-laundering ladder (parse_failure→retry-once→MISSING; substitution gated on outcome===quota_wall only, even a quota_wall on retry returns missing) are all correct. Build+tests green, copies byte-identical. The one gap is real and defeats the bead's whole anti-injection point; it traces to the plan's asymmetric hardening but ships an exploitable vector.
concrete_changes_required: In validateArgs (ms-panel.js ~204-205) add validateShellSafe("slug", a.slug) and validateShellSafe("spec", a.spec) so slug/spec get the same backtick/;/|/&/$(/newline rejection that target and bead_id already receive — they are interpolated into the same shell command string at line 78 and currently pass validatePathElement with metacharacters intact (verified: slug=`x$(id)`, `x;id`, `x|sh` all reach the built command).

Consider also rejecting whitespace in slug/spec/bead_id (as validateTarget already does at line 163): a benign space-containing slug that 110 accepts as one path element is word-split by the workflow's shell string into extra argv tokens (`mindspec panel create foo bar` → slug=foo, stray `bar`). Secondary/optional; the metachar guard above is the load-bearing fix.

Optional note for a follow-up: none of the field guards reject a bare `$` (SHELL_METACHAR_RE only matches `$(`), so `$HOME`-style variable expansion survives on slug/spec/target/bead_id alike; if command strings are shell-executed this is a residual info-leak/expansion vector across all fields. Out of scope for this bead's minimal fix but worth a tracked issue.
findings: HOLE (command injection): slug/spec validated by validatePathElement only (ms-panel.js:204-205), no validateShellSafe, yet interpolated raw at line 78. Empirically slug=`x$(rm -rf ~)`/`x;id`/`x|sh`/backticks all PASS → shell-active in the built command handed to the agent. Asymmetric with target(172)+bead_id(143) which DO get validateShellSafe. Real exploitable vector; the dash guard doesn't cover it.

OK — buildCommand chokepoint matches plan round-3 reconciliation: fixed flags from per-verb template (78-96), leading-dash guard scoped to user VALUES only (64-73), round is an integer so it skips the string dash-check. No regression to blanket-reject and no value can reach a flag position.

OK — ALLOWED_CLI is a static 4-element literal, destructured once (45-52); no concat/.replace/template; `mindspec complete` appears nowhere (test at workflow_test.go:134 pins it over whole content); positive-enumeration test forbids any mindspec/codex literal outside the array. A 5th command cannot be smuggled.

OK — anti-laundering ladder (runCodexSlot 361-387): first exec ok→ok, quota_wall→quota_wall (sub-eligible), parse_failure→single re-serialize retry→ok or MISSING. A rendered-but-malformed verdict can NEVER become quota_wall, and even a quota_wall reported on the RETRY returns `missing` (386), so substitution (Step 4, gated on outcome==="quota_wall", 472) cannot fire on any slot that rendered content. Fail-closed to MISSING → panel verify reports incomplete → gate Blocks.

OK — file paths derive from panelDir captured from the binary's own reported stdout (461), never reconstructed from raw slug; slotId from fixed enum, round is integer → no traversal via slug into a write path.
OK — go build ./... clean; go test ./plugins/... ./internal/setup/... green; diff -q both ms-panel.js copies identical (526 lines each); git status clean at 1ad0d796.
